outcome.note: Measured after training the edited schema in the next iteration.

outcome_decision.reason: True semantic hack risk increased: repeated reward payment, high-reward/low-progress, or shaping-goal mismatch.
True semantic hack risk increased: repeated reward payment, high-reward/low-progress, or shaping-goal mismatch.

quality.interpretation: Avoid repeating unless a later auditor provides strong counter-evidence.

what_failed: Isolated decrease of dominant shaping component without damping other dense contact rewards (lesson_iter_000_to_001).
avoid_next: Solo decrease_weight on a single dense component.
Terminal bonus increase without corresponding dampening of exploitable dense rewards.
recommend_next: Apply coupled rebalancing when dense contact rewards are being exploited: reduce the offending dense weights, massively boost terminal bonus, and optionally add a modest time penalty.
Monitor contact toggles and success rate; if new exploitation emerges, consider stricter stable‑landing conditions.
agent_lessons.applicability_notes: This case shows classic shaping‑goal mismatch; the same pattern should be applied when multiple dense contact‑proximity rewards exist and any of them dominate.

recommendation.applicability_notes: This case shows classic shaping‑goal mismatch; the same pattern should be applied when multiple dense contact‑proximity rewards exist and any of them dominate.

lesson_card.summary: Shaping‑goal mismatch: dense r_landing_quality incentivises unstable contact toggling instead of terminal stable landing. An atomic coupled rebalancing package (reduce r_landing_quality to 0.2, boost r_stable_landing_once to 500, increase energy penalty to 0.05) will shift incentive toward terminal success while maintaining essential stability signals.
